parsed_review.summary: CP00-341 is a descriptor-only intake-core review-closeout-slice pack that closes the RP10 Intake Conflict Engagement descriptor program. Independent read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) confirms the pack-level claim is internally consistent and accurately scoped: production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, implementation_layer='descriptor', status='implemented_pending_review' — no production-ready or enterprise-trust claim is made from local validation. Identity/scope (risk C, 28 units, range RP10.P09.M07.S13-RP10.P09.M10.S04; four sections M07:8/M08:8/M09:8/M10:4; deliverable split claude_review:7/implementation:11/security_audit:6/test:2/ui:2) reconcile across review-baseline.json, manifest plan_binding_snapshot, registry.js INTAKE_CORE_CP341_PACK_BINDING/REQUIREMENTS, intake-core-contract.json projection, the validate script, and node test suite. The descriptor builders (service.js createIntakeCoreCp341ReviewCloseoutSliceCaseSet/Descriptor) emit only frozen objects via freezeCp341Result, which sets ~40 negative-capability flags (no product-state write; no intake/conflict/waiver/engagement/AI runtime dispatch; no permission/audit evaluation; no object storage; no Hermes runtime receipt; no command runtime; no real/fixture data; deny-over-allow; no-leak guards). These are mirrored in the contract no_write_attestation and enforced by validateIntakeCoreCp341Coverage and validateIntakeCoreCp341ReviewCloseoutSliceDescriptor, whose deepEqual contract<->descriptor checks pass per command-evidence (rp10:intake-core:validate and node --test exit 0). Grep confirms no writeFile/appendFile/fetch/child_process/spawn in the changed source or validator; the only fs use is read-only readFile. Authority boundary holds: Claude is read-only with tools Read/Grep/Glob, claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true; Hermes patterns are used only as deterministic control-plane evidence labels (gate H10, receipt_shape descriptor_only...no_write), not product runtime (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false, hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false). CP178+ review hardening is modeled: forbidden_review_evidence rejects failed-authentication, zero-byte, malformed-JSON, fenced-JSON, tool-call-shaped, and missing-raw-output/missing-source-basis outputs, and the readiness auth-preflight passed with non-zero parseable JSON stdout. Handoff closes RP10 and routes to CP00-342 / RP11.P00.M00.S01. Changed-file scope is clean: 8 active implementation refs (index.js unchanged, re-exporting CP341 functions via export*), and unrelated dirty/untracked files (docx, workbook/*, ldip results) are preserved and not attributed to the pack, with no active untracked implementation files omitted. No P0/P1/P2 issues found. Three P3 informational notes (expected pending downstream pipeline artifacts; conditional-but-fail-closed row guards verified safe; transparently-documented sub-range C unit count that errs toward more review rigor) are recorded and do not block pack or goal closeout. Final enterprise-trust/go-live approval remains a human decision outside this read-only receipt.

findings.title: Post-review pipeline artifacts not yet present (expected pre-review state)
findings.details: required_pack_artifacts and the per-unit evidence_refs list docs/closeout-packs/cp00-341/claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json, which are not present yet (Glob shows only manifest.json and command-evidence.json in the pack directory). This is the correct current state: per pack_level_claude_review.hardening_sequence, those three artifacts are produced downstream of this read_only_claude_run step (finding_adjudication, construction_inspection, final_closeout_validation), and pack_level_claude_review.status is 'pending' with valid_review_receipts=[]. Their absence is therefore expected and must not be read as a missing-evidence BLOCK.
findings.recommendation: No action for this review. Ensure the closeout pipeline generates claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json from this verdict before final-closeout validation and commit, so required_pack_artifacts is complete at commit time.

findings.title: Row-level safety guards are conditional but verified fail-closed
findings.details: validateIntakeCoreCp341ReviewCloseoutSliceDescriptor expresses its row safety guards as 'if (rows.X && rows.X.field !== ...)' for claude_review_packet.claude_final_approval_claimed, command_rerun.executes_command_runtime, permission_bypass_questions.permission_bypass_detected, and ui_leak_questions.leak_detected. The row objects carry these fields because INTAKE_CORE_CP341_ROW_EXTRAS spreads INTAKE_CORE_CP340_ROW_EXTRAS -> CP339_ROW_EXTRAS (service.js), which defines those keyed extras; the snake_case keys produced by intakeCoreRowKey match the literal guard keys. node --test (model.test.js:1454-1459) directly asserts m07.command_rerun.executes_command_runtime===false and m08.permission_bypass_questions.permission_bypass_detected===false / ui_leak_questions.leak_detected===false, confirming the keys bind. If a required field were ever absent, the strict comparison evaluates truthy and pushes an error (fails closed). Behavior is safe.
findings.recommendation: No action required. If future hardening is desired, assert the safety fields unconditionally rather than behind the 'rows.X &&' existence guard to make intent explicit; current behavior is fail-closed and correct.

findings.title: Risk-class C pack carries 28 units, below the documented C range (40-150); override is transparent and errs toward more rigor
findings.details: plan_binding_snapshot sets risk_class C with unit_count 28, which is below the pack_count_policy C range (40-150). The deviation is transparently disclosed in the same descriptor: within_default_risk_range=false and override_reason='Generated C pack has 28 units outside 40-150 because ledger-order cohesion is smaller than the default risk range', with deviation_from_plan=false (the pack is generator-produced from the weighted implementation ledger, source_ledger_sha recorded). Assigning the more-rigorous C class to a smaller unit set errs toward more review scrutiny, not less, so it does not weaken any production_ready or review gate. validateIntakeCoreCp341Coverage and the node test both confirm exactly 28 units across the four required sections.
findings.recommendation: No action required; the override is transparently recorded and conservative. If stricter alignment is preferred, the plan generator could classify sub-40-unit ledger-cohesion packs as risk B, but the current C assignment is safe.
